remember: Students will be able to recall the structure of DNA, RNA, and proteins.
Students will be able to identify the types of DNA and RNA.
Students will be able to describe the process of replication and transcription.
understand: Students will be able to explain the concept of mutation and its types.
Students will be able to describe the process of translation.
Students will be able to understand the role of genetic code.
apply: Students will be able to apply the concept of mutation to explain genetic disorders.
Students will be able to describe the role of enzymes in DNA replication and transcription.
Students will be able to explain the process of gene expression.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the relationship between genotype and phenotype.
Students will be able to compare the different models of gene expression.
Students will be able to evaluate the importance of genetic code in protein synthesis.

ncert_objectives: Describe the structure and function of DNA, including the double helix model.
Explain the process of DNA replication and transcription.
Describe the types of RNA and their roles in protein synthesis.
Explain the concept of mutation and its types.
Describe the process of translation and the role of genetic code.
board_exam_objectives: Describe the structure of DNA and its replication process.
Explain the role of mutation in genetic disorders.
Describe the process of gene expression and its regulation.
Compare the different models of gene expression and their implications.
